vocab.meaning: lỗi thời, không phù hợp với thời đại
vocab.example: To some critics, traditional oil portraits in the digital age feel somewhat anachronistic.
Modern art often embraces avant-garde concepts that challenge our perception of reality.

vocab.meaning: sự chân thực về mặt thị giác
vocab.example: Old masters focused heavily on achieving visual verisimilitude to mirror the natural world.

vocab.meaning: chiều sâu về ý niệm/tư tưởng
vocab.example: Unlike classical works, contemporary pieces often prioritize conceptual depth over technical perfection.

vocab.meaning: sự tay nghề/chế tác tỉ mỉ
vocab.example: Renaissance paintings are celebrated for the meticulous craftsmanship evident in every brushstroke.

vocab.meaning: sức mạnh gợi cảm xúc/liên tưởng
vocab.example: Abstract art relies on its evocative power rather than depicting literal subjects.

vocab.meaning: phá vỡ/lật đổ các quy chuẩn truyền thống
vocab.example: Modernist movements sought to subvert traditional norms of symmetry and perspective.

vocab.meaning: thị hiếu/khả năng cảm thụ thẩm mỹ
vocab.example: The shift in art reflects the evolving aesthetic sensibilities of different generations.

vocab.meaning: sự tích hợp đa phương tiện
vocab.example: Modern art frequently utilizes multimedia integration, combining sound, video, and physical objects.
Classic artworks are often steeped in religious or mythological allegorical significance.
